qt: The complex exponential function ez is onto function.
The function ez is an entire function.
Period of the function Cos z is _____________ degree.
$${\text{For the power series }}\sum\limits_{n = 0}^\infty {{Z^n}} {\text{ is equal to __________}{\text{.}}$$
The complex exponential function ez is one-to-one function.
In the context of an infinite power series, what ensures the existence of the corresponding function?
op: The series having an infinite number of terms.
The series having only positive coefficients.

qt: $${\text{The power series }}\sum\limits_{n = 0}^\infty {{C_n}{{(Z - \alpha )}^n}} {\text{converges for all values of z for which |Z - }}\alpha {\text{| _________}}{\text{.}}$$
op: less than equal to radius of convergence

qt: $${\text{For the power series }}\sum\limits_{n = 0}^\infty {(n + 1){Z^n}} {\text{ is equal to _________}}{\text{.}}$$
Which one of the following is the Euler’s formula?
The ratio test for the power series $\sum_{n=0}^{\infty} a_n x^n$ involves computing which of the following limits?
$${\text{In an infinite series of the form }}\sum\limits_{n = 0}^\infty {{C_n}{{(Z - \alpha )}^n}} {\text{ __________ is not a fixed complex number}}{\text{.}}$$
The complex exponential function w = ez is one-to-one if we only consider ___________.
$${\text{The radius of convergence of power series }}\sum\limits_{n = 0}^\infty {{{(\frac{{n + 15}}{{6n + 10}})}^n}{{(Z - 5)}^n}} {\text{ is __________}}{\text{.}}$$
$${\text{The infinite series }}\sum\limits_{n = 0}^\infty {{C_n}{{(Z - \alpha )}^n}} {\text{ defines a function only if it _________}}{\text{.}}$$
How many methods are there to find the radius of convergence of power series?
Which of the following correctly describes the radius of convergence $R$ for the power series $\sum_{n=0}^{\infty} a_n x^n$?
op: $R$ is the distance from the origin to the nearest point where the series diverges.
$R$ is the maximum value of $x$ for which the series converges absolutely.
$R$ is the maximum value of $|x|$ for which the series converges absolutely.
$R$ is the distance from the origin to the nearest point where the series converges.

qt: Why is the infinite series used in constructing complex valued functions?
Period of the function ez is _____________ degree.
Under what condition does an infinite power series define a function?
op: When the series has an odd number of terms.
When the series has a constant term.
